prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like trying to navigate an unfamiliar city by bus at night, armed only with a map drawn in the dark. You know the general direction to your destination, but the roads are winding and the stops are unmarked. You occasionally catch a glimpse of a familiar landmark in the dim light, offering a glimmer of recognition. With each ride, your confidence grows as you learn to read the signs in the dark, finding your way with increased ease and velocity.
